mnemonics: Carriers Sense Multiple Accesses Collide Detect
keyPoints: The data link layer is responsible for node-to-node communication on the same network segment.
It handles framing, physical addressing (MAC), error control, flow control, and access control.
Protocols like CSMA/CD (for Ethernet) and PPP are key examples of data link layer protocols.
commonMistakes: Confusing MAC addresses (Data Link Layer) with IP addresses (Network Layer).
Assuming all data link protocols handle multiple access (e.g., PPP is for point-to-point links only).
Forgetting that flow control mechanisms (like sliding windows) operate at this layer.
